name: ResNet50
fullName: Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition
description: ResNet50 is a seminal convolutional neural network utilizing residual skip connections to facilitate backpropagation in deep architectures. This addresses the vanishing gradient problem, allowing stable weight updates across 50 layers.

educationalNote.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
educationalNote.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent fully connected layers.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions. Resizing inputs to 224x224 is required prior to forward propagation.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors in standard CNN dense layers.

calculationSteps.label: Kernel Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 64 independent filters of size 7x7 operating over 3 input channels. No bias is used because it is immediately followed by Batch Normalization.

educationalNote.detailed: Slides 64 different 7x7 kernels with a stride of 2 to extract general contours while shrinking spatial grid sizes.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Shrinks resolution from 224x224 to 112x112, reducing downstream computation.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Early pooling and large stride convolutions preserve core shapes while minimizing compute requirements.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNote.detailed: Batch Normalization shifts activations to mean 0 and variance 1 across batch dimensions to avoid internal covariate shifts.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: BN layers are essential to stabilize gradient updates in deep CNNs.

educationalNote.detailed: Calculates f(x) = max(0, x) element-wise. Keeps active regions positive and sets negative features to zero.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: ReLU allows learning complex non-linear combinations of edges.

educationalNote.summary: Pads borders to control MaxPooling dimensions.
educationalNote.detailed: Adds zero borders around the 112x112 feature map to expand it to 114x114, ensuring MaxPooling dimensions fit correctly.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Zero padding avoids border information loss.

educationalNote.detailed: Extracts the maximum active pixel in overlapping 3x3 cells with stride 2.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: MaxPooling reduces resolution while filtering peak activations.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 1x1 convolution mapping 64 channels to 64 bottleneck channels.

educationalNote.detailed: This 1x1 filter reduces channels to 64 prior to the expensive 3x3 convolution, saving parameters.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Controls computation scales in deep bottleneck sandwiches.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: 1x1 convolutions act as spatial-preserving channel projection blocks.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Stabilizes inputs for subsequent spatial convs.
Introduces activation non-linearity.

calculationSteps.label: Kernel Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 3x3 spatial filter operating on 64 channels. Stride of 1 handles spatial downsampling.

educationalNote.detailed: Extracts patterns (corners, junctions) on the bottleneck channel representation. If stride is 2, it reduces spatial height and width by half.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Performs spatial convolutions on the reduced volume to save weights.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Maintains spatial scale consistency.
Non-linear activation.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 1x1 convolution projecting channels from 64 back to 256 dimensions.

educationalNote.detailed: Expands output from 64 channels back to 256 channels, restoring visual descriptors.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Expansion restores the high-dimensional capacity of features before residual adding.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Ensures stable signals for addition.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Projects shortcut channels from 64 to 256 to match spatial sizes.

educationalNote.detailed: Ensures the shortcut tensor matches the main branch shape 256 for element-wise addition.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Used only when spatial grid or channels change between blocks.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Normalizes the projected skip connection.

educationalNote.summary: Performs element-wise residual addition: F(x) + x.
educationalNote.detailed: Adds the output of the convolutional sandwich to the skip connection. This enables gradients to flow directly backwards.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: The core mathematical mechanism that avoids vanishing gradients in deep ResNets.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Identity loops guarantee stable gradient updates.
Final activation step of the bottleneck block.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 1x1 convolution mapping 256 channels to 64 bottleneck channels.

educationalNote.detailed: This 1x1 filter reduces channels to 64 prior to the expensive 3x3 convolution, saving parameters.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Controls computation scales in deep bottleneck sandwiches.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: 1x1 convolutions act as spatial-preserving channel projection blocks.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 1x1 convolution projecting channels from 256 back to 1024 dimensions.

educationalNote.detailed: Expands output from 256 channels back to 1024 channels, restoring visual descriptors.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Expansion restores the high-dimensional capacity of features before residual adding.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Ensures stable signals for addition.

educationalNote.summary: Performs element-wise residual addition: F(x) + x.
educationalNote.detailed: Adds the output of the convolutional sandwich to the skip connection. This enables gradients to flow directly backwards.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: The core mathematical mechanism that avoids vanishing gradients in deep ResNets.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Identity loops guarantee stable gradient updates.
Final activation step of the bottleneck block.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 1x1 convolution mapping 1024 channels to 256 bottleneck channels.

educationalNote.detailed: This 1x1 filter reduces channels to 256 prior to the expensive 3x3 convolution, saving parameters.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Controls computation scales in deep bottleneck sandwiches.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: 1x1 convolutions act as spatial-preserving channel projection blocks.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Stabilizes inputs for subsequent spatial convs.
Introduces activation non-linearity.

calculationSteps.label: Kernel Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 3x3 spatial filter operating on 256 channels. Stride of 1 handles spatial downsampling.

educationalNote.detailed: Extracts patterns (corners, junctions) on the bottleneck channel representation. If stride is 2, it reduces spatial height and width by half.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Performs spatial convolutions on the reduced volume to save weights.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Projects shortcut channels from 1024 to 2048 to match spatial sizes.

educationalNote.detailed: Ensures the shortcut tensor matches the main branch shape 2048 for element-wise addition.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Used only when spatial grid or channels change between blocks.

educationalNote.summary: Performs element-wise residual addition: F(x) + x.
educationalNote.detailed: Adds the output of the convolutional sandwich to the skip connection. This enables gradients to flow directly backwards.

Final activation step of the bottleneck block.

educationalNote.summary: Averages spatial dimensions to form a global feature vector.
educationalNote.detailed: Computes the spatial average of each of the 2,048 feature channels. Avoids parameter bloat associated with flattening.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: GAP acts as a global regularizer and reduces downstream parameters by 98%.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Maps the 2048 pooled features to 1000 ImageNet classifications.

calculationSteps.label: Biases
calculationSteps.explanation: One learnable bias parameter per output category.

educationalNote.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Classification decision layer.

groups.name: Input Stem
groups.description: Stem layers downsampling the high-res input image to 56x56 prior to deep stage routing.

groups.name: Stage 2 Block 1
groups.description: Bottleneck residual block: Conv 1x1 → 3x3 → 1x1 with projection skip connection.

groups.name: Stage 2 Block 2
groups.description: Bottleneck residual block: Conv 1x1 → 3x3 → 1x1 with identity skip connection.

groups.name: Stage 2 Block 3
groups.description: Bottleneck residual block: Conv 1x1 → 3x3 → 1x1 with identity skip connection.

groups.name: Stage 3 Block 1
groups.description: Bottleneck residual block: Conv 1x1 → 3x3 → 1x1 with projection skip connection.

groups.name: Stage 3 Block 2
groups.description: Bottleneck residual block: Conv 1x1 → 3x3 → 1x1 with identity skip connection.

groups.name: Stage 3 Block 3
groups.description: Bottleneck residual block: Conv 1x1 → 3x3 → 1x1 with identity skip connection.

groups.name: Stage 3 Block 4
groups.description: Bottleneck residual block: Conv 1x1 → 3x3 → 1x1 with identity skip connection.

groups.name: Stage 4 Block 1
groups.description: Bottleneck residual block: Conv 1x1 → 3x3 → 1x1 with projection skip connection.

groups.name: Stage 4 Block 2
groups.description: Bottleneck residual block: Conv 1x1 → 3x3 → 1x1 with identity skip connection.

groups.name: Stage 4 Block 3
groups.description: Bottleneck residual block: Conv 1x1 → 3x3 → 1x1 with identity skip connection.

groups.name: Stage 4 Block 4
groups.description: Bottleneck residual block: Conv 1x1 → 3x3 → 1x1 with identity skip connection.

groups.name: Stage 4 Block 5
groups.description: Bottleneck residual block: Conv 1x1 → 3x3 → 1x1 with identity skip connection.

groups.name: Stage 4 Block 6
groups.description: Bottleneck residual block: Conv 1x1 → 3x3 → 1x1 with identity skip connection.

groups.name: Stage 5 Block 1
groups.description: Bottleneck residual block: Conv 1x1 → 3x3 → 1x1 with projection skip connection.

groups.name: Stage 5 Block 2
groups.description: Bottleneck residual block: Conv 1x1 → 3x3 → 1x1 with identity skip connection.

groups.name: Stage 5 Block 3
groups.description: Bottleneck residual block: Conv 1x1 → 3x3 → 1x1 with identity skip connection.

groups.name: Global Pooling & Classification
groups.description: Global average pooling averaging spatial channels to a 2048 feature vector, followed by predictions.
